pages.full_text:
Westbury Court Garden originally laid out 1696-17[illegible]
Restored with original plante 1967 N.T.
(Before Capability Brown)    lovely tulips &
On home for tea.              apple trees
photo_captions: Enjoy Westbury Court Garden — Rediscover a formal Dutch water garden (National Trust brochure cover, glued in)
pages.notes: Page is dominated by a glued-in National Trust brochure/leaflet for Westbury Court Garden. Handwritten annotations appear below the brochure. The date '1696-17[illegible]' refers to the garden's original layout date; the final two digits of the end year are cut off or unclear. 'plante' likely = 'plants' (original spelling preserved). No handwritten page number visible on this side.

pages.full_text:
Friday 29th May.
Simon, Eve, Tinder left at 6 for Coughton
Court. We are in a farmer's field. ****
Good foot path walks.
__Saturday__
We walked about 3 miles by the river
& left Tinder in the van. Lunch in the
courtyard block, then explored the house
& garden [?for] of the Throckmorton family.
lived here for 600 years. Catholic. One
woman had 19 children who all survived
Owen priest's hole. Lovely house & garden.
Of the 13 people implicated in the gun-
powder plot, 7 were related to the
Throckmortons.
We suppered in the van. It was cold.
__Sunday__ We went to see a nearby NT [illegible]
dovecote. [?If] with ladder intact, it was
amazing. The bird perches were staggered
in the way they pointed.
We walked by the river &
got home for lunch.
pages.notes: Page number 70 visible top-right corner. 'Tinder' appears to be the family dog (left in the van). 'Owen priest's hole' refers to Father John Gerard's priest hole at Coughton Court (associated with the Throckmorton family and the Gunpowder Plot). 'NT' = National Trust. 'dovecote' — word before it is largely illegible, possibly 'kiln' or a place name. The year is uncertain: 'Friday 29th May' matches 2009 or 2015 within the volume's 2003–2019 range; 2009 assigned as best guess given earlier placement in the volume (handwritten page 70), but confidence is low. '[?for]' — small word between 'garden' and 'of' is unclear. Some decorative stamp/sticker marks visible (asterisks noted as '****' in text, and a wheat-ear motif at page foot which is a printed diary decoration, not handwritten).
